cases.prompt: In this Doors app, add a debounced search input that updates results as the user types without sending every keystroke.
grading_assertions: Uses AInput or another appropriate Doors event attr rather than browser-only JavaScript for the core state update.
Uses ScopeDebounce, optionally combined with ScopeLatest, to control request scheduling.
Stores the search query in a Source or updates a Door explicitly; does not assume a virtual DOM re-render.
Uses the handler ctx for Source updates and returns the correct handler continuation boolean.

cases.prompt: Add a reactive counter component to a Doors page using Source so only the displayed count updates when the button is clicked.
grading_assertions: Creates or reuses doors.Source[int] for the count.
Renders the displayed count through Bind or Effect instead of relying on full component re-rendering.
Updates the Source from the click handler using the handler context.
Keeps component state ownership consistent with the existing project style.
Audits existing reactive state usage and preserves or improves narrow ownership/bind boundaries instead of introducing broad rerenders.

cases.prompt: Add a static repeated content section with several blocks, each with title, body text or markup, and a small list of items, to an existing Doors page.
grading_assertions: Models each repeated block as a component struct with fields and a Main method rather than loose per-field variables rendered in a parent template.
Keeps the template shape inside the component and passes data through composite literals, constructor/factory values, or a top-level slice of component values.
Renders slices of component values with explicit GoX for/range syntax when multiple blocks are needed, leaving room for per-item wrappers/classes/attrs.

Does not recommend direct ~(contentBlocks) for typed component slices such as []ContentBlock; only treats direct slice rendering as acceptable when the slice is intentionally typed as []any or []gox.Comp.
Uses fields such as string, []string, or any for variable text/markup and does not mix hardcoded list items with unrelated placeholder variables in the parent template.

cases.prompt: Add a /reports route with an optional page query parameter and active navigation link in a Doors app.
grading_assertions: Extends or reuses the existing typed path model instead of inventing an unrelated router.
Represents the optional query parameter with the path model conventions documented by the skill.
Uses doors.Route or related Route primitives in the rendered component tree.
Uses ALink with Active configuration for current-page styling.
Communicates and follows the Doors routing ladder: top-level RouteModel/path switch, narrower Route primitives for branches, then params/query binds only where consumed.
Distinguishes Route from Bind/Effect: Route keeps the branch while the match outcome stays the same; Bind/Effect rerender on subscribed value changes.
Does not bind the whole path model at the page shell merely to branch on the section; uses Route primitives for the minimal page switch and handles path-derived data down-tree.

cases.prompt: Refactor a Doors app whose top-level Path has Section plus detail ID, query, page, and filter fields. Keep the route model as one source of truth, but make the route hierarchy follow Doors' ladder: top-level path-model switch, narrower path-model branches, then params/query updates only in fragments that read them.
grading_assertions: Keeps one typed Path source of truth while routing or deriving smaller values for branches, params, query, page, and filters.
Uses doors.Route/RouteModel for URL model selection and path.Route with RouteMatch, RouteDerive, RouteValue, or RouteDefault* for route dispatch instead of path.Bind.
Explains or implements that Route dispatch keeps the active branch while the active match stays the same, while Bind/Effect need derived state to stay targeted.
Does not render a Page(path Source[Path]) pattern that binds the whole Path and branches only on p.Section.
Keeps the top-level route switch minimal: it chooses the active page/segment, or handles truly global effects, and does not bind query, page, or filter fields there.
Uses RouteDerive, RouteMatch.Comp, or RouteDefaultComp when a branch only needs a smaller value or no routed value, rather than passing the full path by habit.
Ensures query, page, or filter changes within a section do not remount or rerender the entire page shell.
Binds or effects narrower path-derived values inside the active page fragments that actually depend on those values.

cases.prompt: Refactor a dashboard Source[DashboardState] with selected tab, filters, counters, and detail data so changing a filter does not rerender the whole dashboard shell or unrelated panels.
grading_assertions: Audits the existing reactive state layout before editing and identifies broad Bind/Effect boundaries that would rerender unrelated UI.
Keeps one coherent source of truth while deriving narrower Source or Beam values for tabs, filters, counters, and detail data.
Uses Route/RouteBeam for branch keys such as selected tab when branch identity matters, even when that key is derived from a larger source.
Uses Bind/Effect only on derived values inside fragments that actually consume those values.
Does not split state into unrelated sources merely to imitate a virtual DOM; derives narrow views from the owning state.

cases.prompt: Review this existing Doors diff for reactive-state best-practice issues: broad Source bindings, Effect boundaries around whole panels, tab switches implemented with Bind, and state split into many unrelated sources.
grading_assertions: Audits the existing project code rather than only answering the requested local change.
Flags broad Bind/Effect boundaries that rerender unrelated UI when a derived value would be narrower.
Recommends Route/RouteBeam for branch keys where the active branch should stay mounted while the match is unchanged, including derived keys.
Distinguishes healthy single-source derivation from unnecessary splitting into many unrelated Sources.
Prioritizes findings by behavioral impact on Doors' no-virtual-DOM update model.

cases.prompt: Create or refactor a Doors app entry point to mount middleware, serve static files, configure CSP/timeouts, and keep routing inside the returned root component.
grading_assertions: Uses doors.NewApp with a per-request page factory that returns the root component.
Attaches middleware or static file serving through the documented App APIs.
Keeps app routing in the rendered component tree instead of turning NewApp's factory into the router.
Uses the documented configuration options for CSP, timeouts, server ID, or local cookie behavior.

cases.prompt: Add a lazy details panel to a Doors component that loads content only after a click and updates just that panel.
grading_assertions: Uses doors.Door when Bind/Effect alone is not the right fit for explicit partial updates.
Mounts the Door in the template with the documented proxy or render form.
Calls Door update methods from a handler or runtime-managed context, not from context.Background().
Accounts for lifecycle behavior when a dynamic parent unmounts or the Door is replaced.

cases.prompt: Add email/password login and logout to a Doors app with session-backed auth state and a protected dashboard route; use the Doors skill's auth, routing, events, and state reference files before answering.
grading_assertions: Uses session storage or server-owned state for auth identity and does not trust route params or hidden inputs.
Uses ASubmit or ARawSubmit according to the form decoding needs.
Updates route or reactive auth state after successful login/logout.
Checks authorization while deciding what to render and rechecks at the service/database operation level when permissions can change.
Initializes any local Source fields before Bind/Update and uses GoX ~(...) interpolation rather than JSX-style {expr}; does not render a LoginPage with a nil message Source field.
Does not call path.Update, auth.Update, or other state mutations from render paths such as Bind callbacks, Effect bodies, or GoX ~{...} blocks to redirect.
Uses Route primitives for route dispatch with auth binding inside protected branches, or binds auth first only when the whole app shell is auth-gated and routes inside the authorized branch.
Uses RouteMatch(...).Comp(...) or RouteDefaultComp(...) for static/login branches that do not need the routed source, instead of .Source(...) with an ignored or forwarded path source.
Does not use whole-path Bind, path.Get inside an auth-only Bind, or pass a full doors.Source[Path] into any component that does not read or write the route.

cases.prompt: Show live job status in a Doors component by polling a backend every few seconds while the component is mounted.
grading_assertions: Uses Doors-scoped background work for component or instance lifetime where appropriate.
Pushes backend updates into Source or another documented reactive primitive.
Uses the correct Doors runtime context for state updates and lifecycle-bound work.
Avoids unsafe mutation of plain shared Go state from the polling goroutine.

Uses GoX control flow documented by the bundled GoX reference; does not invent unsupported placeholder forms such as ~(switch ...) and uses either if/else-if or a ~func inline expression whose switch cases return.

cases.prompt: Add a form that uploads a CSV file, validates it on the server, and then offers a generated report download from a Doors page.
grading_assertions: Chooses ARawSubmit or another documented raw form path when multipart/file handling needs custom parsing.
Treats uploaded filenames, content type, and form values as client-controlled input.
Validates CSV by parsing/sniffing content rather than accepting or rejecting solely by filename suffix or MIME type.
Uses ResourceBytes, ResourceHook, ResourceHandler, or another documented resource shape for the generated download.
Sets a safe download name/content type and chooses cache/private behavior intentionally.
Does not derive the generated download name directly from the uploaded filename unless it is explicitly sanitized to a server-owned safe name.
Uses indication or actions only for client feedback, not as the source of durable server state.

cases.prompt: Serve embedded images and a CSS file from a Doors app, with cache headers and CSP configured correctly.
grading_assertions: Chooses the appropriate resource constructor or app middleware for embedded/static assets.
Attaches CSS through the documented stylesheet resource pattern.
Sets cache behavior explicitly when it matters.
Accounts for CSP when external resources or scripts/styles are involved.

cases.prompt: Add a browser-side toast event from a Doors handler and wire a small JavaScript module that can call back into Go. The page may render multiple copies of this component, so any JavaScript element lookup must avoid ID collisions.
grading_assertions: Uses Doors actions such as ActionEmit/Call for imperative browser behavior instead of replacing reactive UI updates.
Uses the documented managed script or module shape for browser-side JavaScript.
Uses AHook or the documented JS-to-Go hook API when JavaScript must call back into Go.
Attaches AHook/AData to the managed script that calls $hook/$data, or imports module code from that managed script.

Uses document.currentScript.parentElement.querySelector/querySelectorAll for colocated inline-script DOM islands, falling back to document.currentScript.closest(...) when the target wrapper is not the direct parent, or passes a selector-compatible ID through markup/data when a real cross-script handle is required: doors.IDRand() for unique per-render IDs or doors.IDString(stableUniqueValue) for deterministic IDs from component data such as title, slug, or URL.
Does not hardcode reusable component IDs such as modal/root/toast target IDs in a way that collides when the component is rendered more than once.
Accounts for CSP/esbuild configuration when modules or external scripts are introduced.

cases.prompt: Set a dynamic page title, return 404 for an unknown route, and sync a disabled attribute across a group of controls in a Doors page.
grading_assertions: Uses the documented head/title/meta API rather than hand-writing unrelated browser JavaScript.
Sets initial HTTP status from the rendered route fallback.
Uses AShared when one attribute should be shared across multiple elements.
Keeps route fallback behavior separate from authorization decisions.

Uses GoX control flow documented by the bundled GoX reference; does not invent unsupported placeholder forms such as ~(switch ...) and uses either if/else-if or a ~func inline expression whose switch cases return.
Does not call AShared Enable/Disable from a render block merely to correct initial attribute state.

cases.prompt: Add two related save controls where normal edits may debounce, the final save must wait for pending edits, stale responses should not control the UI, and the user sees pending/error feedback.
grading_assertions: Uses documented scope composition such as ScopeDebounce, ScopeLatest, ScopeSerial, ScopeBlocking, ScopeFrame, or JoinScopes instead of hand-rolled request scheduling.
Does not treat client-side scopes as data-safety or authorization guarantees.
Uses indicators for request lifecycle feedback and ActionIndicate/OnError only when timed or error feedback is needed.
Keeps durable UI/data state in Source/Beam or server-owned storage, not in temporary client indication.
Initializes any Source/Beam fields before Bind/Update rather than relying on zero-value handles.

cases.prompt: Review this Doors diff for security issues around route params, active links, hidden inputs, and auth checks.
grading_assertions: Treats URL, path params, query params, form values, and event data as client-controlled.
Flags any use of route matches, active links, or hidden inputs as authorization proof.
Recommends server-owned state or database checks for trust-bearing values.
Notes when handler-level rechecks are needed because permissions can change after render.

cases.prompt: Edit a Doors .gox component to add a conditional empty state with plain text in both branches, a loop of results, a status branch that would normally tempt a switch placeholder, and a click handler.
grading_assertions: Reads the bundled Doors-specific GoX LLM reference, references/00-gox.md, before editing or reviewing Doors code.
Edits the .gox source and does not manually edit generated .x.go files.
Uses GoX control flow and Doors event attrs with valid modifier or proxy syntax.
Does not wrap plain text branches in empty fragments such as <>text</> inside ~(if); writes text directly or uses real markup when markup is needed.
Uses documented if/for placeholders, or a ~func inline expression whose switch cases return, instead of inventing unsupported placeholder forms.

cases.prompt: Edit a Doors .gox component so visible copy includes a literal tilde character and an unmatched closing brace.
grading_assertions: Reads references/00-gox.md before editing GoX syntax.
Renders a literal tilde as ~"~" rather than leaving raw template text that GoX parses as an escape.
Renders an unmatched closing brace as ~"}" rather than leaving raw template text that GoX treats as unbalanced syntax.
Edits the .gox source and regenerates generated files instead of hand-editing .x.go.

cases.prompt: Review and fix a Doors .gox diff that imports goxx for class composition, wraps a Bind with parallel rendering, and uses a proxy helper to add data-testid through a Button component.
grading_assertions: Removes goxx from the Doors app and uses Doors-native helpers instead.
Replaces class composition with doors.Class and uses it correctly as a class value, attribute modifier, or proxy.
Uses doors.ProxyMod or a built-in Doors attr proxy when an attribute must pass through a component/container to the first real element.
Uses doors.Parallel only for independent static render fragments with expensive DB/API work, not in front of Source/Beam Bind, Effect, Route, or doors.Door rendering.

cases.prompt: Design tests for a Doors app change that adds pure validation logic, a click handler that updates Source-backed UI, an ALink navigation flow with browser back, and a repository query. Include how you would use Rod during development if no browser controller is available.
grading_assertions: Uses ordinary Go unit or integration tests for pure validation and repository/query logic rather than Rod.
Uses Rod e2e against a real doors.App for hook-driven Source updates, Bind/Effect rerenders, ALink navigation, and browser back behavior.
Tests runtime behavior through real browser actions and visible DOM/URL postconditions rather than calling handlers directly, mutating Source from the test, or only checking href/initial render.
Builds or recommends a small Rod harness with Rod's default Chromium download behavior, CI NoSandbox only when needed, timeout-bound navigation, page/server cleanup, and user-visible assertions.
Treats temporary Rod checks as an investigation aid for browser/runtime behavior, converts meaningful checks into committed e2e tests, and deletes one-off probes.
